channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Papua New Guinea gains a team in Australian rugby league in diplomatic push aimed at curbing China
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Papua New Guinea will gain its own team in Australia’s rugby league in a soft diplomacy deal announced Thursday linked to limiting Chinese influence in the South Pacific.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: FIFA confirms Saudi Arabia as 2034 World Cup host despite human rights concerns
item.description: ZURICH (AP) — Saudi Arabia was officially confirmed Wednesday by FIFA as host of the 2034 World Cup in men’s soccer, giving the oil-rich kingdom its biggest prize yet for massive spending on global sports driven by Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM DUNBAR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korean president says martial law was an act of governance and denies rebellion charges
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea's president defended his martial law decree as an act of governance and denied rebellion charges Thursday, rejecting the opposition-led impeachment attempts against him and investigations into last week's move.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UN General Assembly demands ceasefire in Gaza and backs UN agency helping Palestinian refugees
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The U.N. General Assembly overwhelmingly approved resolutions Wednesday demanding an immediate ceasefire in Gaza and backing the U.N. agency for Palestinian refugees that Israel has moved to ban.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Middle East latest: UN General Assembly demands a ceasefire in Gaza
item.description: The U.N. General Assembly overwhelmingly approved resolutions Wednesday demanding an immediate ceasefire in Gaza and expressing support for the U.N. agency for Palestinian refugees that Israel has moved to ban.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lawmakers call for intelligence assessment on ending U.S. support for Ukraine
item.description: Lawmakers want the key intelligence chiefs to assess U.S. security risks if America stops providing weapons to Ukraine and Russian wins the war. The lawmakers are pressing for a provision to be added to the National Defense Authorization Act, which the House is expected to vote on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: America's closest ally in Syria is losing ground as a new order takes shape
item.description: The jihadi rebels who toppled Syrian President Bashar Assad say they want to build a unified, inclusive country. But after nearly 14 years of civil war, putting that ideal into practice will not be easy.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: The world’s largest Christmas tree farm in Oregon sells nearly 1 million trees annually
item.description: Oregon produces more Christmas trees than any other U.S. state, with 4.7 million harvested every year. The state produces 33% of the United States' Christmas trees.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hundreds of tractors block central London streets as farmers protest tax change
item.description: An inheritance tax of up to 20 percent will be applied to British farms in 2026. Hundreds of farmers protested the new policy by blocking the streets of London with tractors Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gabbard tests trust of allies as Trump’s potential intel director
item.description: Controversy over President-elect Trump’s pick for intelligence chief, former Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (Hawaii), is raising uncomfortable questions for America’s closest allies over whether the U.S. will remain a trusted partner for sharing critical, sensitive information.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Ex-defense chief in South Korea tried to kill himself after being arrested over martial law
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s previous defense minister was stopped from attempting suicide while in detention over last week’s martial law declaration, officials said Wednesday, as President Yoon Suk Yeol’s office resisted a police attempt to search the compound.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG and HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Brazil’s President Lula to undergo a new procedure after initial surgery for brain bleed
item.description: Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva will undergo a new procedure on Thursday following up on an unplanned surgery earlier this week for a brain bleed.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: North Korea condemns South Korea as 'fascist dictatorship' after martial law scare
item.description: North Korean dictator Kim Jong Un denounced South Korea as a 'fascist dictatorship' following President Yoon Suk Yeol's failed attempt to impose martial law.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Scholz sets Germany on course for an early election as he requests a confidence vote next week
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Chancellor Olaf Scholz formally set Germany on course for an early election Wednesday by requesting a confidence vote in parliament next week.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supporters of Bangladesh Nationalist Party hold protest march as tensions continue with India
item.description: DHAKA, Bangladesh (AP) — Thousands of members of youth and student bodies belonging to the Bangladesh Nationalist Party on Wednesday held a road march procession from Bangladesh’s capital toward its eastern border with India to protest against an attack on a diplomatic mission earlier this month and
item.dc:creator: JULHAS ALAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mike Huckabee 'commends' Biden support for Israel's self-defense
item.description: Israel said Tuesday it had bombed more than 350 military sites in Syria during the previous 48 hours.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, The Hill

item.title: Taiwan demands that China end its military activity in nearby waters
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — Taiwan demanded Wednesday that China end its ongoing military activity in nearby waters, which it said is unilaterally undermining peace and stability and disrupting international shipping and trade.
item.dc:creator: TAIJING WU and KEN MORITSUGU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Humanitarian aid to northern Gaza mostly blocked for the last 2 months, UN says
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — Humanitarian aid to n orth Gaza, where Israel launched a ground offensive on Oct. 6, has largely been blocked for the past 66 days, the United Nations said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rights group demands probe of ex-governor, 2 army officers in Congo for possible humanitarian crimes
item.description: DAKAR, Senegal (AP) — Amnesty International called for an investigation Wednesday into a former governor and two top army officials in Congo for “possible crimes against humanity” during a deadly crackdown on demonstrators last year protesting the U.N. peacekeeping mission in the country.
item.dc:creator: MARK BANCHEREAU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mike Huckabee 'commends' Biden support for Israel's self-defense
item.description: Israel said Tuesday it had bombed more than 350 military sites in Syria during the previous 48 hours.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: US defense secretary in Japan to support alliance as Osprey aircraft safety causes concern
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — U.S. Defense Secretary Lloyd Austin met with officials in Japan on Tuesday to reaffirm the importance of their alliance and Washington's commitment to regional security as threats rise from China and North Korea. Austin also stressed that U.S.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel bombs hundreds of sites across Syria as army pushes into border zone
item.description: Israel said Tuesday it had bombed more than 350 military sites in Syria during the previous 48 hours, targeting “most of the strategic weapons stockpiles” in the country.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump names Kimberly Guilfoyle ambassador to Greece
item.description: President-elect Trump named Kimberly Guilfoyle as ambassador to Greece in his upcoming administration. Guilfoyle is a former Fox News personality and has been a staple of the Trump family for years since she previously was in a relationship with Donald Trump Jr. The two reportedly have broken up.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Almost half of Ukrainians now support Trump: Poll
item.description: Almost half of Ukrainians now support President-elect Trump as he is set to take the Oval Office next month, according to a poll released on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Netanyahu wants to establish ‘relations’ with Syria but says Israel will attack it if becomes a threat
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said he wants to establish a relationship with the incoming Syrian government but warned Israel will protect itself amid strikes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 1,500-year-old Ten Commandments tablet set for auction
item.description: The tablet has twenty lines of text but only nine of the commandments, with "Thou shalt not take the name of the Lord in vain" omitted.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Brazilian President Lula in ICU after surgery to drain head hemorrhage, hospital says
item.description: Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva is recovering in an ICU after undergoing surgery to drain an intracranial hemorrhage following a fall at home in October.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Journalists' rights group says 104 media workers killed this year
item.description: More than half of the journalists and media workers killed perished during the Israel-Hamas war in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US hostage envoy in Beirut to seek information on missing journalist Austin Tice
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. government's top hostage negotiator is in Beirut in hopes of collecting information on the whereabouts of Austin Tice, an American journalist missing in Syria for 12 years, the State Department said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Syrian community in Chicago celebrates end of Assad regime
item.description: It was a sea of green flags on Michigan Avenue Sunday as hundreds of Chicagoans celebrated the end of the regime in Syria.
item.dc:creator: Emmy Victor, NewsNation

item.title: Notre Dame opens to the public for first time since 2019 fire
item.description: Later on Sunday, an evening Mass will welcome members of the public who secured reservations last week.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Syrian government falls in stunning end to 50-year rule of Assad family
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — The Syrian government fell early Sunday in a stunning end to the 50-year rule of the Assad family after a sudden rebel offensive sprinted across government-held territory and entered the capital in 10 days.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE and ZEINA KARAM, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Syrian insurgents reach gates of Damascus, threatening decades-long Assad regime
item.description: Syrian insurgents have reached the gates of the nation's capital, shortly after Bashar al-Assad's government abandoned the key city of Homs.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump urges US to stay out of Syrian civil war, blaming Obama for failure as Islamists close in on capital
item.description: Amid the rapid offensive of Islamist rebels in their campaign to dislodge Syrian dictator Assad, President-elect Donal Trump has urged non-intervention by the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Notre Dame Cathedral reopens its doors for first time since devastating 2019 fire
item.description: France's iconic Notre Dame Cathedral is formally reopening its doors on Saturday for the first time since a devastating fire nearly destroyed the 861-year-old landmark in 2019.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Adamson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Donald Trump's incoming team met with Ukraine officials
item.description: Trump's team is trying to bring Ukraine and Russia to the negotiating table quickly, potentially through diplomatic coercion.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation
